docs.title: GoJira
docs.text:
GoJira is a Go SDK and CLI for Jira that provides:

  • REST API client (rest/) - wrapper around go-jira with additional utilities
  • XML parser (xml/) - parse Jira XML exports when API access is unavailable
  • JQL builder (root package) - programmatically construct JQL queries
  • CLI tool (cmd/gojira/) - command-line interface optimized for AI agents and humans

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Multiple output formats: JSON, Table, and TOON (Token-Optimized Object Notation)
  • Flexible authentication: Environment variables, goauth credential files, or CLI flags
  • AI-agent friendly: Non-interactive mode, structured output, consistent exit codes
  • Lightweight core: Root package has no external dependencies

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text: Package Description Dependencies gojira JQL builder, config, constants None (lightweight) gojira/rest REST API client go-jira SDK gojira/xml XML export parser None gojira/web URL helpers None

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
CLISDK 
# Search issues
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND status = Open"

# Get issue details
gojira get ISSUE-123

# Show statistics
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by status --format table

import "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"

client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
    "https://your-instance.atlassian.net",
    "your-email@example.com",
    "your-api-token",
    false,
)

issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues("project = FOO", false)

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
1. Automate Jira operations - Search, update, and export issues via CLI
  2. Build custom integrations - Use the SDK to integrate Jira with other systems
  3. Generate reports - Export issues to JSON/XLSX for analysis
  4. AI agent workflows - Token-optimized output for LLM consumption
  5. Parse XML exports - Access Jira data when API is unavailable

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
• Installation - Install the CLI and SDK
  • Quick Start - Get up and running in minutes
  • CLI Reference - Full command documentation
  • SDK Guide - Using GoJira as a library

docs.title: CLI Installation
docs.text:
Install the gojira command-line tool:

go install github.com/grokify/gojira/cmd/gojira@latest

Verify the installation:

gojira version

docs.title: SDK Installation
docs.text:
Add GoJira as a dependency to your Go project:

go get github.com/grokify/gojira

Import the packages you need:

import (
    "github.com/grokify/gojira"       // JQL builder, config
    "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"  // REST API client
    "github.com/grokify/gojira/xml"   // XML parser
)

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Go 1.21 or later
  • Jira Cloud or Jira Server instance
  • API token (for Jira Cloud) or username/password (for Jira Server)

docs.title: Getting an API Token
docs.text:
For Jira Cloud, you need an API token instead of your password:

  1. Go to Atlassian API Tokens
  2. Click Create API token
  3. Give it a descriptive name (e.g., "gojira CLI")
  4. Copy the token - you won't see it again

Keep your token secure

API tokens provide full access to your Jira account. Never commit them to version control or share them publicly.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start - Configure authentication and run your first commands
  • Authentication - Detailed authentication options

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get up and running with GoJira in minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.21 or later installed
  • Access to a Jira instance (Cloud or Server)
  • API token or credentials

docs.title: 1. Install the CLI
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/gojira/cmd/gojira@latest

docs.title: 2. Configure Authentication
docs.text:
Set environment variables for your Jira instance:

export JIRA_URL=https://your-instance.atlassian.net
export JIRA_USER=your-email@example.com
export JIRA_TOKEN=your-api-token

Add to your shell profile

Add these exports to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc to persist them.

docs.title: 3. Search for Issues
docs.text:
# Search for open issues in a project
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND status = Open"

# Get the first 10 results
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --max 10

# Output as human-readable table
gojira search --jql "assignee = currentUser()" --table

docs.title: 4. Get Issue Details
docs.text:
# Get a single issue
gojira get ISSUE-123

# Get multiple issues
gojira get ISSUE-123 ISSUE-456

# Include changelog
gojira get ISSUE-123 --expand

docs.title: 5. View Statistics
docs.text:
# Count issues by status
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by status --format table

# Count by assignee
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by assignee

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text:
GoJira supports three output formats:

Format Flag Use Case JSON --json Default, machine-readable Table --table Human-readable TOON --toon Token-optimized for LLMs

Example:

# JSON output (default)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --json

# Table output
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --table

# TOON output (efficient for AI agents)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --toon

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Authentication - Configure goauth credentials
  • CLI Reference - Full command documentation
  • JQL Examples - Common JQL patterns

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: The gojira command-line interface provides commands for searching, retrieving, and managing Jira issues.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/gojira/cmd/gojira@latest

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description search Search issues with JQL get Get one or more issues by key patch Update issue fields export Export issues to JSON or XLSX fields List and filter custom fields stats Show issue statistics grouped by field version Show version information

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text:
These flags are available on all commands:

Flag Short Description --json -j Output as JSON (default) --table -t Output as human-readable table --toon Output as TOON (Token-Optimized Object Notation) --creds-file Path to goauth credentials file --account Account key in credentials file --quiet -q Suppress non-essential output

docs.title: JSON (default)
docs.text:
Machine-readable JSON output, ideal for piping to jq or programmatic processing:

gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --json | jq '.[].key'

docs.title: Table
docs.text:
Human-readable ASCII table:

gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --table

Output:

KEY        TYPE    STATUS    SUMMARY
FOO-123    Bug     Open      Fix login issue
FOO-124    Story   Done      Add user profile

docs.title: TOON
docs.text:
Token-Optimized Object Notation - compact format optimized for LLM consumption:

gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --toon

TOON format uses abbreviated keys and is approximately 8x more token-efficient than JSON.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
The CLI authenticates in this order:

  1. CLI flags: --creds-file and --account
  2. Environment variables: JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_TOKEN
  3. goauth file: ~/.config/goauth/credentials.json

See Authentication for details.

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 Error (authentication failure, API error, invalid arguments)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Search with environment variables
export JIRA_URL=https://your-instance.atlassian.net
export JIRA_USER=your-email@example.com
export JIRA_TOKEN=your-api-token
gojira search --jql "project = FOO"

# Search with credentials file
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --creds-file ~/.config/goauth/creds.json --account myaccount

# Quiet mode (suppress progress messages)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" -q

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: GoJira supports multiple authentication methods to connect to your Jira instance.

docs.title: Authentication Priority
docs.text:
The CLI checks authentication sources in this order:

  1. CLI flags (--creds-file, --account)
  2. Environment variables (JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_TOKEN)
  3. goauth credentials file (~/.config/goauth/credentials.json)

docs.title: Method 1: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Set these environment variables:

export JIRA_URL=https://your-instance.atlassian.net
export JIRA_USER=your-email@example.com
export JIRA_TOKEN=your-api-token

This is the recommended method for:

  • Local development
  • CI/CD pipelines
  • Docker containers
  • AI agent integrations

Non-interactive

Environment variables enable fully non-interactive operation, ideal for automation.

docs.title: Method 2: goauth Credentials File
docs.text: GoJira integrates with goauth for credential management.

docs.title: Default Location
docs.text: ~/.config/goauth/credentials.json

docs.title: File Format
docs.text:
{
  "credentials": {
    "jira-prod": {
      "service": "jira",
      "type": "basic",
      "basic": {
        "serverURL": "https://your-instance.atlassian.net",
        "username": "your-email@example.com",
        "password": "your-api-token"
      }
    },
    "jira-staging": {
      "service": "jira",
      "type": "basic",
      "basic": {
        "serverURL": "https://staging.atlassian.net",
        "username": "your-email@example.com",
        "password": "your-staging-token"
      }
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Using a Specific Account
docs.text:
# Use account key from default credentials file
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --account jira-prod

# Use custom credentials file
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --creds-file ~/my-creds.json --account myaccount

docs.title: Interactive Selection
docs.text: If no --account is specified, goauth will interactively prompt you to select an account.

docs.title: Jira Cloud
docs.text:
1. Go to Atlassian API Tokens
  2. Click Create API token
  3. Give it a descriptive name (e.g., "gojira CLI")
  4. Copy the token immediately

docs.title: Jira Server / Data Center
docs.text: Use your Jira password or create a personal access token in your Jira Server settings.

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
Keep credentials secure

  • Never commit credentials to version control
  • Use environment variables in CI/CD
  • Restrict API token permissions when possible
  • Rotate tokens periodically

docs.title: Using .envrc with direnv
docs.text:
For project-specific credentials, use direnv:

# .envrc (add to .gitignore)
export JIRA_URL=https://your-instance.atlassian.net
export JIRA_USER=your-email@example.com
export JIRA_TOKEN=your-api-token

Then run:

direnv allow

docs.title: "authentication failed" Error
docs.text:
1. Verify your credentials are correct
  2. Check that JIRA_URL includes https://
  3. Ensure your API token hasn't expired
  4. Verify you have access to the Jira project

docs.title: Testing Authentication
docs.text:
# Test with a simple query
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --max 1

# Check version (no auth required)
gojira version

docs.title: export
docs.text: Export Jira issues to JSON or XLSX format.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gojira export [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Description --jql JQL query to search issues --keys Comma-separated issue keys to export --json Output JSON file path --xlsx Output XLSX file path --from-json Read issues from existing JSON file instead of querying --include-parents Include parent issues in export --sheet Sheet name for XLSX export (default: "issues")

Plus global flags.

docs.title: Export to JSON
docs.text:
# Export search results to JSON
gojira export --jql "project = FOO" --json issues.json

# Export specific issues
gojira export --keys FOO-1,FOO-2,FOO-3 --json output.json

docs.title: Export to Excel
docs.text:
# Export to XLSX
gojira export --jql "project = FOO AND status = Open" --xlsx report.xlsx

# Custom sheet name
gojira export --jql "project = FOO" --xlsx report.xlsx --sheet "Open Issues"

docs.title: Export Both Formats
docs.text:
# Export to both JSON and XLSX
gojira export --jql "project = FOO" --json backup.json --xlsx report.xlsx

docs.title: Include Parent Issues
docs.text:
When exporting sub-tasks or stories linked to epics, include the parent issues:

gojira export --jql "project = FOO AND issuetype = Sub-task" --include-parents --xlsx subtasks.xlsx

docs.title: Convert JSON to XLSX
docs.text:
If you have an existing JSON export, convert it to XLSX:

gojira export --from-json issues.json --xlsx report.xlsx

docs.title: JSON Output
docs.text:
The JSON file contains the full issue data including all fields:

{
  "issues": [
    {
      "key": "FOO-123",
      "fields": {
        "summary": "Fix login bug",
        "status": {"name": "Open"},
        ...
      }
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: XLSX Output
docs.text:
The Excel file contains a table with columns:

  • Key
  • Type
  • Status
  • Priority
  • Summary
  • Assignee
  • Created
  • Updated
  • And more...

docs.title: Backup Issues
docs.text:
# Regular backup of project issues
gojira export --jql "project = FOO" --json "backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

docs.title: Generate Reports
docs.text:
# Sprint report
gojira export --jql "project = FOO AND sprint = 'Sprint 42'" --xlsx sprint-42.xlsx

# Unresolved bugs report
gojira export --jql "project = FOO AND type = Bug AND resolution is EMPTY" --xlsx bugs.xlsx

docs.title: Data Analysis
docs.text:
# Export for external analysis
gojira export --jql "project = FOO AND created >= -30d" --json recent.json

# Then use with jq, Python, etc.
cat recent.json | jq '.issues | length'

docs.title: fields
docs.text: List and filter Jira custom fields.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gojira fields [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Description --id Filter by field ID(s), comma-separated --name Filter by name (partial match) --name-exact Filter by exact name --custom-only Show only custom fields (exclude system fields) --epic-link Show Epic Link field --json Output as JSON --table Output as table (default)

Plus global flags.

docs.title: List All Fields
docs.text:
# List all custom fields as table
gojira fields

# List as JSON
gojira fields --json

docs.title: Filter by ID
docs.text:
# Get specific field
gojira fields --id customfield_10001

# Get multiple fields
gojira fields --id customfield_10001,customfield_10002

docs.title: Search by Name
docs.text:
# Partial match search
gojira fields --name "Epic"

# Exact name match
gojira fields --name-exact "Epic Link"

docs.title: Custom Fields Only
docs.text:
# Exclude system fields
gojira fields --custom-only

docs.title: Get Epic Link Field
docs.text:
Find the Epic Link field (useful for linking issues to epics):

gojira fields --epic-link

docs.title: Table Format
docs.text:
ID                  NAME              TYPE         CUSTOM
customfield_10001   Epic Link         string       true
customfield_10002   Story Points      number       true
customfield_10003   Sprint            array        true

docs.title: JSON Format
docs.text:
[
  {
    "id": "customfield_10001",
    "name": "Epic Link",
    "custom": true,
    "schema": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  }
]

docs.title: Find Field IDs for Queries
docs.text:
# Find the Sprint field ID
gojira fields --name Sprint

# Use in search
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND cf[10003] is not EMPTY"

docs.title: Discover Available Fields
docs.text:
# See all custom fields your Jira has
gojira fields --custom-only | head -20

docs.title: Script Integration
docs.text:
# Get Epic Link field ID
EPIC_FIELD=$(gojira fields --epic-link --json | jq -r '.[0].id')
echo "Epic Link field: $EPIC_FIELD"

docs.title: get
docs.text: Get one or more Jira issues by their keys.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gojira get <issue-key> [issue-key...] [flags]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description issue-key One or more Jira issue keys (e.g., FOO-123)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Default Description --expand false Expand changelog and other fields --fields Comma-separated list of fields to include

Plus global flags.

docs.title: Single Issue
docs.text:
# Get a single issue
gojira get FOO-123

# Human-readable output
gojira get FOO-123 --table

docs.title: Multiple Issues
docs.text:
# Get multiple issues
gojira get FOO-123 FOO-456 FOO-789

# Output as JSON array
gojira get FOO-123 FOO-456 --json

docs.title: With Changelog
docs.text:
# Include issue history
gojira get FOO-123 --expand

docs.title: JSON Format
docs.text:
[
  {
    "key": "FOO-123",
    "fields": {
      "summary": "Fix login bug",
      "status": {
        "name": "Open"
      },
      "issuetype": {
        "name": "Bug"
      },
      "priority": {
        "name": "High"
      },
      "assignee": {
        "displayName": "John Doe"
      },
      "created": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000+0000",
      "updated": "2024-01-16T14:20:00.000+0000"
    }
  }
]

docs.title: Table Format
docs.text:
KEY        TYPE    STATUS    PRIORITY    ASSIGNEE     SUMMARY
FOO-123    Bug     Open      High        John Doe     Fix login bug

docs.title: Scripting
docs.text:
# Get issue and extract status
STATUS=$(gojira get FOO-123 -q | jq -r '.[0].fields.status.name')
echo "Issue status: $STATUS"

# Check if issue is closed
if [[ $(gojira get FOO-123 -q | jq -r '.[0].fields.status.name') == "Closed" ]]; then
  echo "Issue is closed"
fi

docs.title: AI Agent Workflows
docs.text:
# Get issue details for LLM context
gojira get FOO-123 --toon

docs.title: patch
docs.text: Update one or more fields on a Jira issue.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gojira patch <issue-key> [flags]

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description issue-key The Jira issue key to update (e.g., FOO-123)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Description --set Set field value (format: field=value). Can be repeated. --add-label Add a label to the issue. Can be repeated. --remove-label Remove a label from the issue. Can be repeated. --json Raw JSON body for complex updates --dry-run Show request body without executing --show-after Show issue after update

Plus global flags.

docs.title: Setting Fields
docs.text:
# Set a simple field
gojira patch FOO-123 --set summary="New summary"

# Set multiple fields
gojira patch FOO-123 --set summary="New title" --set priority=High

# Set a custom field
gojira patch FOO-123 --set customfield_10001="custom value"

docs.title: Managing Labels
docs.text:
# Add a label
gojira patch FOO-123 --add-label bug

# Add multiple labels
gojira patch FOO-123 --add-label bug --add-label urgent

# Remove a label
gojira patch FOO-123 --remove-label obsolete

# Add and remove in one command
gojira patch FOO-123 --add-label reviewed --remove-label needs-review

docs.title: Complex Updates with JSON
docs.text:
# Use JSON for complex field structures
gojira patch FOO-123 --json '{"fields":{"summary":"New title","labels":["bug","urgent"]}}'

# Update nested fields
gojira patch FOO-123 --json '{"fields":{"assignee":{"name":"jdoe"}}}'

docs.title: Dry Run
docs.text:
Preview what would be sent without making changes:

gojira patch FOO-123 --set summary="Test" --dry-run

Output:

Would PATCH FOO-123 with:
{
  "fields": {
    "summary": {
      "value": "Test"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Show Updated Issue
docs.text:
# Show issue after update
gojira patch FOO-123 --set summary="Updated" --show-after

docs.title: Standard Fields
docs.text: Field Example summary --set summary="New summary" description --set description="New description" priority --set priority=High

docs.title: Custom Fields
docs.text:
Use the field ID (e.g., customfield_10001). Use gojira fields to find field IDs:

# List custom fields
gojira fields --custom-only

# Use in patch
gojira patch FOO-123 --set customfield_10001="value"

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
On success:

Successfully updated FOO-123 (status: 204)

On failure, an error message with the HTTP status code is displayed.

docs.title: search
docs.text: Search for Jira issues using JQL (Jira Query Language).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gojira search --jql <query> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Short Default Description --jql (required) JQL query string --max -m 50 Maximum number of results --all -a false Retrieve all results (paginate automatically) --fields -f Comma-separated list of fields to include

Plus global flags.

docs.title: Basic Search
docs.text:
# Search for open issues in a project
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND status = Open"

# Search assigned to current user
gojira search --jql "assignee = currentUser()"

docs.title: Limiting Results
docs.text:
# Get first 10 results
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --max 10

# Get all results (may be slow for large datasets)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --all

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text:
# JSON output (default)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --json

# Human-readable table
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --table

# Token-optimized for LLMs
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --toon

docs.title: Piping to jq
docs.text:
# Extract just the keys
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" | jq -r '.[].key'

# Get summaries
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" | jq -r '.[] | "\(.key): \(.fields.summary)"'

docs.title: Quiet Mode
docs.text:
# Suppress progress messages (useful for scripting)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" -q

docs.title: JSON Format
docs.text:
[
  {
    "key": "FOO-123",
    "fields": {
      "summary": "Fix login bug",
      "status": {
        "name": "Open"
      },
      "issuetype": {
        "name": "Bug"
      }
    }
  }
]

docs.title: Table Format
docs.text:
KEY        TYPE    STATUS    SUMMARY
FOO-123    Bug     Open      Fix login bug
FOO-124    Story   Done      Add user profile

docs.title: JQL Tips
docs.text:
See JQL Examples for common query patterns.

# Complex queries
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND status in (Open, 'In Progress') AND priority = High"

# Date filtering
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND created >= -7d"

# Text search
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND text ~ 'error'"

docs.title: stats
docs.text: Show aggregate statistics for issues grouped by a field.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: gojira stats --jql <query> --by <field> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Default Description --jql (required) JQL query to search issues --by (required) Field to group by (see Grouping Fields) --format toon Output format: toon, json, or table

Plus global flags.

docs.title: Grouping Fields
docs.text: Field Description status Issue status (Open, In Progress, Done, etc.) type Issue type (Bug, Story, Task, etc.) priority Issue priority (High, Medium, Low, etc.) assignee Assigned user project Project key resolution Resolution status customfield_XXXXX Any custom field by ID

docs.title: Count by Status
docs.text:
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by status --format table

Output:

VALUE          COUNT      %
Open              45   30.0%
In Progress       30   20.0%
Done              75   50.0%
------         ------  ------
TOTAL            150  100.0%

docs.title: Count by Type
docs.text: gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by type --format table

docs.title: Count by Priority
docs.text: gojira stats --jql "project = FOO AND status != Done" --by priority --format table

docs.title: Count by Assignee
docs.text: gojira stats --jql "project = FOO AND status = 'In Progress'" --by assignee --format table

docs.title: Count by Custom Field
docs.text:
# Find field ID first
gojira fields --name "Team"

# Then use it
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by customfield_10005 --format table

docs.title: TOON (default)
docs.text:
Token-optimized format for LLMs:

gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by status

docs.title: JSON
docs.text:
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by status --format json

Output:

{
  "field": "status",
  "total": 150,
  "results": [
    {"value": "Done", "count": 75},
    {"value": "Open", "count": 45},
    {"value": "In Progress", "count": 30}
  ]
}

docs.title: Table
docs.text: gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by status --format table

docs.title: Sprint Planning
docs.text:
# Status breakdown for sprint
gojira stats --jql "sprint = 'Sprint 42'" --by status --format table

# Type breakdown
gojira stats --jql "sprint = 'Sprint 42'" --by type --format table

docs.title: Workload Analysis
docs.text:
# Issues per assignee
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO AND status != Done" --by assignee --format table

docs.title: Project Health
docs.text:
# Unresolved issues by priority
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO AND resolution is EMPTY" --by priority --format table

docs.title: Script Integration
docs.text:
# Get count of open bugs
OPEN_BUGS=$(gojira stats --jql "project = FOO AND type = Bug AND status = Open" --by status --format json -q | jq '.total')
echo "Open bugs: $OPEN_BUGS"

docs.title: AI Agents Guide
docs.text: GoJira is designed to work well with AI agents like Claude Code. This guide covers best practices for AI-agent workflows.

docs.title: Why GoJira for AI Agents?
docs.text:
1. Non-interactive operation: All auth via environment variables or files
  2. Structured output: JSON and TOON formats for reliable parsing
  3. Consistent exit codes: 0 for success, 1 for errors
  4. Token efficiency: TOON format reduces token consumption by ~8x
  5. Quiet mode: Suppress progress messages for cleaner output

docs.title: TOON Format
docs.text:
TOON (Token-Optimized Object Notation) is a compact format designed for LLM consumption:

# JSON output (verbose)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --json

# TOON output (compact)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --toon

docs.title: TOON Example
docs.text:
JSON: 

{
  "field": "status",
  "total": 150,
  "results": [
    {"value": "Done", "count": 75},
    {"value": "Open", "count": 45}
  ]
}

TOON: 

f:status|t:150|r:[{v:Done,n:75},{v:Open,n:45}]

The TOON format uses abbreviated keys (f for field, t for total, etc.) and minimal punctuation.

docs.title: Environment Setup
docs.text:
Set credentials via environment variables for non-interactive operation:

export JIRA_URL=https://your-instance.atlassian.net
export JIRA_USER=your-email@example.com
export JIRA_TOKEN=your-api-token

docs.title: 1. Search and Parse Issues
docs.text:
# Search for issues (quiet mode, TOON output)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND status = Open" --toon -q

# Search with JSON for detailed parsing
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --json -q | jq '.[].key'

docs.title: 2. Get Issue Details
docs.text:
# Get single issue
gojira get FOO-123 --toon -q

# Get multiple issues
gojira get FOO-123 FOO-456 --json -q

docs.title: 3. Analyze Issue Distribution
docs.text:
# Status breakdown
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by status --format json -q

# Type breakdown
gojira stats --jql "project = FOO" --by type --format json -q

docs.title: 4. Update Issues
docs.text:
# Add label
gojira patch FOO-123 --add-label reviewed -q

# Set field
gojira patch FOO-123 --set priority=High -q

docs.title: 5. Export for Analysis
docs.text:
# Export to JSON
gojira export --jql "project = FOO" --json issues.json -q

# Export to XLSX
gojira export --jql "project = FOO" --xlsx report.xlsx -q

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Check exit codes for automation:

if gojira search --jql "project = FOO" -q > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Search succeeded"
else
    echo "Search failed"
fi

docs.title: Token Optimization Tips
docs.text:
1. Use TOON format: --toon uses ~8x fewer tokens than JSON
  2. Use quiet mode: -q suppresses progress messages
  3. Limit results: --max 10 for quick checks
  4. Use stats: Get summaries instead of raw issues

docs.title: Example: Efficient Sprint Status Check
docs.text:
# Inefficient (returns full issue data)
gojira search --jql "sprint = 'Sprint 42'" --json

# Efficient (returns summary counts)
gojira stats --jql "sprint = 'Sprint 42'" --by status --format toon -q

docs.title: SDK Integration
docs.text:
For programmatic AI agent integration:

package main

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "os"

    "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"
)

func main() {
    // Create client from environment
    client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
        os.Getenv("JIRA_URL"),
        os.Getenv("JIRA_USER"),
        os.Getenv("JIRA_TOKEN"),
        false,
    )
    if err != nil {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", err)
        os.Exit(1)
    }

    // Search and output JSON
    issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues("project = FOO", false)
    if err != nil {
        fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: %v\n", err)
        os.Exit(1)
    }

    // Output as JSON for LLM parsing
    data, _ := json.Marshal(issues)
    fmt.Println(string(data))
}

docs.title: Security Considerations
docs.text:
1. Use environment variables for credentials, not CLI flags
  2. Use goauth files with appropriate file permissions
  3. Never log credentials in AI agent outputs
  4. Limit API token scope when possible

docs.title: JQL Examples
docs.text: Common JQL (Jira Query Language) patterns for use with the GoJira CLI and SDK.

docs.title: By Project
docs.text:
# Single project
gojira search --jql "project = FOO"

# Multiple projects
gojira search --jql "project in (FOO, BAR, BAZ)"

docs.title: By Status
docs.text:
# Single status
gojira search --jql "status = Open"

# Multiple statuses
gojira search --jql "status in (Open, 'In Progress', 'In Review')"

# Exclude statuses
gojira search --jql "status not in (Done, Closed)"

docs.title: By Type
docs.text:
# Bugs only
gojira search --jql "issuetype = Bug"

# Multiple types
gojira search --jql "issuetype in (Bug, Task, Story)"

docs.title: By Assignee
docs.text:
# Current user
gojira search --jql "assignee = currentUser()"

# Specific user
gojira search --jql "assignee = 'john.doe'"

# Unassigned
gojira search --jql "assignee is EMPTY"

docs.title: AND
docs.text: gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND status = Open AND type = Bug"

docs.title: OR
docs.text: gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND (status = Open OR status = 'In Progress')"

docs.title: Relative Dates
docs.text:
# Created in last 7 days
gojira search --jql "created >= -7d"

# Updated in last 24 hours
gojira search --jql "updated >= -1d"

# Due in next 3 days
gojira search --jql "due <= 3d"

docs.title: Absolute Dates
docs.text:
# Created after date
gojira search --jql "created >= '2024-01-01'"

# Created between dates
gojira search --jql "created >= '2024-01-01' AND created <= '2024-01-31'"

docs.title: Date Functions
docs.text:
# Created this week
gojira search --jql "created >= startOfWeek()"

# Due this month
gojira search --jql "due >= startOfMonth() AND due <= endOfMonth()"

# Updated this year
gojira search --jql "updated >= startOfYear()"

docs.title: Full Text
docs.text:
# Search all text fields
gojira search --jql "text ~ 'error'"

# Exact phrase
gojira search --jql 'text ~ "connection timeout"'

docs.title: Summary Only
docs.text: gojira search --jql "summary ~ 'login'"

docs.title: Description Only
docs.text: gojira search --jql "description ~ 'reproduction steps'"

docs.title: By Priority
docs.text:
# High priority
gojira search --jql "priority = High"

# High or Critical
gojira search --jql "priority in (High, Highest, Critical)"

docs.title: By Resolution
docs.text:
# Unresolved
gojira search --jql "resolution is EMPTY"

# Resolved
gojira search --jql "resolution is not EMPTY"

# Won't Fix
gojira search --jql "resolution = 'Won\\'t Fix'"

docs.title: Sprint
docs.text:
# Current sprint
gojira search --jql "sprint in openSprints()"

# Specific sprint
gojira search --jql "sprint = 'Sprint 42'"

# Future sprints
gojira search --jql "sprint in futureSprints()"

docs.title: Fix Version
docs.text:
# Specific version
gojira search --jql "fixVersion = '1.0'"

# No fix version
gojira search --jql "fixVersion is EMPTY"

# Released versions
gojira search --jql "fixVersion in releasedVersions()"

docs.title: Labels
docs.text:
# Has label
gojira search --jql "labels = bug"

# Has any of these labels
gojira search --jql "labels in (bug, urgent, production)"

# No labels
gojira search --jql "labels is EMPTY"

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
# In component
gojira search --jql "component = Backend"

# Multiple components
gojira search --jql "component in (Backend, API)"

docs.title: Custom Fields
docs.text:
# By custom field ID
gojira search --jql "cf[10001] = 'Value'"

# By custom field name (if unique)
gojira search --jql "'Story Points' >= 5"

# Custom field is empty
gojira search --jql "cf[10001] is EMPTY"

docs.title: Epics
docs.text:
# Issues in an epic
gojira search --jql "'Epic Link' = FOO-100"

# Issues with no epic
gojira search --jql "'Epic Link' is EMPTY"

docs.title: Sub-tasks
docs.text:
# Sub-tasks of an issue
gojira search --jql "parent = FOO-123"

# All sub-tasks in project
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND issuetype = Sub-task"

docs.title: ORDER BY
docs.text:
# Order by priority (descending)
gojira search --jql "project = FOO ORDER BY priority DESC"

# Order by created date
gojira search --jql "project = FOO ORDER BY created ASC"

# Multiple order fields
gojira search --jql "project = FOO ORDER BY priority DESC, created ASC"

docs.title: Limiting Results
docs.text:
# First 10 results
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --max 10

# All results
gojira search --jql "project = FOO" --all

docs.title: Open High-Priority Bugs
docs.text: gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND type = Bug AND priority in (High, Highest) AND status != Done"

docs.title: My Overdue Tasks
docs.text: gojira search --jql "assignee = currentUser() AND due < now() AND resolution is EMPTY"

docs.title: Recently Updated in Sprint
docs.text: gojira search --jql "sprint in openSprints() AND updated >= -1d ORDER BY updated DESC"

docs.title: Blockers and Critical Issues
docs.text: gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND (priority = Blocker OR labels = critical) AND resolution is EMPTY"

docs.title: Stale Issues
docs.text:
# Not updated in 30 days, still open
gojira search --jql "project = FOO AND updated <= -30d AND status not in (Done, Closed)"

docs.title: JQL Builder (SDK)
docs.text:
Use the SDK's JQL builder for programmatic query construction:

import "github.com/grokify/gojira"

jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    TypesIncl:    [][]string{{"Bug"}},
    StatusesExcl: [][]string{{"Done", "Closed"}},
}

query := jql.String()
// project = 'FOO' AND type = 'Bug' AND status NOT IN ('Done', 'Closed')

See JQL Builder for more details.

docs.title: SDK Guide
docs.text: GoJira provides a Go SDK for interacting with the Jira REST API. The SDK is organized into packages with clear dependency boundaries.

docs.title: Package Structure
docs.text: Package Import Description gojira github.com/grokify/gojira JQL builder, config, constants (no external deps) rest github.com/grokify/gojira/rest REST API client (requires go-jira) xml github.com/grokify/gojira/xml XML export parser (no external deps) web github.com/grokify/gojira/web URL helpers (no external deps)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "log"

    "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"
)

func main() {
    // Create client with basic auth
    client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
        "https://your-instance.atlassian.net",
        "your-email@example.com",
        "your-api-token",
        false, // don't load custom fields on init
    )
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Search issues
    issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues("project = FOO AND status = Open", false)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Print results
    for _, issue := range issues {
        fmt.Printf("%s: %s\n", issue.Key, issue.Fields.Summary)
    }
}

docs.title: Client
docs.text:
The rest.Client is your entry point to the API. It provides access to service APIs:

  • client.IssueAPI - Issue operations (search, get, update)
  • client.CustomFieldAPI - Custom field operations
  • client.BacklogAPI - Backlog operations

See Client for details.

docs.title: Issues
docs.text:
Issues are returned as rest.Issues (a slice of jira.Issue). Use IssuesSet for advanced operations:

issuesSet, err := issues.IssuesSet(nil)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Get counts by status
counts := issuesSet.CountsByStatus()

See Issues for details.

docs.title: JQL Builder
docs.text:
The root package provides a JQL builder for constructing queries programmatically:

import "github.com/grokify/gojira"

jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO", "BAR"}},
    StatusesIncl: [][]string{{"Open", "In Progress"}},
}

query := jql.String()
// Result: "project IN ('FOO', 'BAR') AND status IN ('Open', 'In Progress')"

See JQL Builder for details.

docs.title: Authentication Options
docs.text:
// Option 1: Direct credentials
client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(url, user, token, false)

// Option 2: From goauth credentials file
client, err := rest.NewClientGoauthBasicAuthFile(
    "~/.config/goauth/credentials.json",
    "jira-prod",
    false,
)

// Option 3: Interactive selection from goauth CLI
client, err := rest.NewClientFromGoauthCLI(true, false)

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All operations return errors that should be checked:

issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues(jql, false)
if err != nil {
    // Handle error (authentication, network, API errors)
    return err
}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Client - Creating and configuring clients
  • Issues - Working with issues
  • JQL Builder - Building JQL queries

docs.title: Client
docs.text: The rest.Client provides access to the Jira REST API. It wraps the go-jira library with additional utilities.

docs.title: From Direct Credentials
docs.text:
import "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"

client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
    "https://your-instance.atlassian.net", // Server URL
    "your-email@example.com",              // Username
    "your-api-token",                       // API token/password
    false,                                   // Load custom fields on init
)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

docs.title: From goauth Credentials File
docs.text:
client, err := rest.NewClientGoauthBasicAuthFile(
    "~/.config/goauth/credentials.json", // Credentials file path
    "jira-prod",                          // Account key
    false,                                 // Load custom fields
)

docs.title: From Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a helper to read from environment:

import (
    "os"
    "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"
)

func NewClientFromEnv() (*rest.Client, error) {
    return rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
        os.Getenv("JIRA_URL"),
        os.Getenv("JIRA_USER"),
        os.Getenv("JIRA_TOKEN"),
        false,
    )
}

docs.title: Interactive Selection
docs.text:
Use goauth CLI for interactive account selection:

client, err := rest.NewClientFromGoauthCLI(
    true,  // Include accounts on error
    false, // Load custom fields
)

docs.title: Client Structure
docs.text:
type Client struct {
    Config         *gojira.Config
    HTTPClient     *http.Client
    JiraClient     *jira.Client
    Logger         *slog.Logger
    BacklogAPI     *BacklogService
    CustomFieldAPI *CustomFieldService
    IssueAPI       *IssueService
    CustomFieldSet *CustomFieldSet
}

docs.title: IssueAPI
docs.text:
The most commonly used service for issue operations:

// Search issues
issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues("project = FOO", false)

// Get single issue
ctx := context.Background()
issue, err := client.IssueAPI.Issue(ctx, "FOO-123", nil)

// Get multiple issues
issues, err := client.IssueAPI.Issues(ctx, []string{"FOO-1", "FOO-2"}, nil)

// Update issue
resp, err := client.IssueAPI.IssuePatch(ctx, "FOO-123", patchBody)

docs.title: CustomFieldAPI
docs.text:
Access custom field definitions:

// Get all custom fields
fields, err := client.CustomFieldAPI.GetCustomFields()

// Get Epic Link field
epicField, err := client.CustomFieldAPI.GetCustomFieldEpicLink()

docs.title: BacklogAPI
docs.text:
Backlog operations:

// Get backlog for a board
backlog, err := client.BacklogAPI.Backlog(boardID)

docs.title: Loading Custom Fields
docs.text:
Custom fields can be loaded during client initialization or later:

// Load during init
client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(url, user, token, true)

// Or load later
err := client.LoadCustomFields()

// Access loaded fields
if client.CustomFieldSet != nil {
    field, ok := client.CustomFieldSet.ByID("customfield_10001")
}

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
The client includes configuration from the root package:

// Access config
serverURL := client.Config.ServerURL

// Modify config
client.Config.ServerURL = "https://new-instance.atlassian.net"

docs.title: Logging
docs.text:
Set a logger for debug output:

import "log/slog"

client.Logger = slog.Default()

docs.title: Low-Level Access
docs.text:
For operations not covered by service APIs, access the underlying clients:

// go-jira client
jiraClient := client.JiraClient

// Raw HTTP client
httpClient := client.HTTPClient

docs.title: Example: Complete Workflow
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "log"

    "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"
)

func main() {
    // Create client
    client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
        "https://your-instance.atlassian.net",
        "your-email@example.com",
        "your-api-token",
        true, // Load custom fields
    )
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Search issues
    issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues("project = FOO AND status = Open", false)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Found %d issues\n", len(issues))

    // Get details for first issue
    if len(issues) > 0 {
        ctx := context.Background()
        issue, err := client.IssueAPI.Issue(ctx, issues[0].Key, nil)
        if err != nil {
            log.Fatal(err)
        }
        fmt.Printf("Issue: %s - %s\n", issue.Key, issue.Fields.Summary)
    }
}

docs.title: Issues
docs.text: The SDK provides several types for working with Jira issues.

docs.title: rest.Issues
docs.text:
A slice of jira.Issue from the go-jira library:

issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues("project = FOO", false)
// issues is rest.Issues ([]jira.Issue)

for _, issue := range issues {
    fmt.Printf("%s: %s\n", issue.Key, issue.Fields.Summary)
}

docs.title: rest.IssueMore
docs.text:
A wrapper around jira.Issue with convenience methods:

import "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"

issue := issues[0]
im := rest.NewIssueMore(&issue)

// Convenience accessors
key := im.Key()
summary := im.Summary()
status := im.StatusName()
typeName := im.TypeName()
assignee := im.AssigneeName()
resolution := im.Resolution()

docs.title: rest.IssuesSet
docs.text:
A structured set of issues with aggregation and filtering:

// Create from Issues
issuesSet, err := issues.IssuesSet(nil)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Get counts
countsByStatus := issuesSet.CountsByStatus()
countsByType := issuesSet.CountsByType(true, true)
countsByProject := issuesSet.CountsByProject()

// Filter
filtered := issuesSet.FilterByStatus("Open")

docs.title: Basic Search
docs.text:
issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues(
    "project = FOO AND status = Open", // JQL query
    false,                              // Retrieve all (paginate)
)

docs.title: Search with Pagination
docs.text:
// Retrieve all matching issues (handles pagination internally)
issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues(jql, true)

docs.title: Search to IssuesSet
docs.text: issuesSet, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssuesSet("project = FOO")

docs.title: Single Issue
docs.text:
ctx := context.Background()
issue, err := client.IssueAPI.Issue(ctx, "FOO-123", nil)

docs.title: With Options
docs.text:
opts := &rest.GetQueryOptions{
    ExpandChangelog: true,  // Include issue history
}
issue, err := client.IssueAPI.Issue(ctx, "FOO-123", opts)

docs.title: Multiple Issues
docs.text:
keys := []string{"FOO-123", "FOO-456", "FOO-789"}
issues, err := client.IssueAPI.Issues(ctx, keys, nil)

docs.title: Aggregations
docs.text:
issuesSet, _ := issues.IssuesSet(nil)

// Count by standard fields
byStatus := issuesSet.CountsByStatus()     // map[string]uint
byType := issuesSet.CountsByType(true, true)
byProject := issuesSet.CountsByProject()

// Count by custom field
byCF, err := issuesSet.CountsByCustomFieldValues("customfield_10001")

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text:
// Filter by status
open := issuesSet.FilterByStatus("Open")

// Filter by type
bugs := issuesSet.FilterByType("Bug")

// Filter by custom criteria
filtered := issuesSet.Filter(func(im *rest.IssueMore) bool {
    return im.Priority() == "High"
})

docs.title: Export to Table
docs.text:
tbl, err := issuesSet.TableDefault(nil, true, "Initiative", []string{})
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Write to file
err = tbl.WriteXLSX("output.xlsx", "Issues")

docs.title: Parent Issues
docs.text:
Include parent issues (epics, initiatives):

err := client.IssueAPI.IssuesSetAddParents(issuesSet)
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

// Access parents
if issuesSet.Parents != nil {
    parentKeys := issuesSet.Parents.Keys()
}

docs.title: From JSON
docs.text:
issuesSet, err := rest.IssuesSetReadFileJSON("backup.json")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}

docs.title: Issue Fields
docs.text:
Access fields through the jira.Issue.Fields struct:

issue := issues[0]

// Standard fields
summary := issue.Fields.Summary
description := issue.Fields.Description
status := issue.Fields.Status.Name
issueType := issue.Fields.Type.Name
priority := issue.Fields.Priority.Name
assignee := issue.Fields.Assignee.DisplayName
created := issue.Fields.Created
updated := issue.Fields.Updated

// Custom fields (via Unknowns map)
if val, ok := issue.Fields.Unknowns["customfield_10001"]; ok {
    // Handle custom field value
}

docs.title: Example: Issue Report
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "log"

    "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"
)

func main() {
    client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
        "https://your-instance.atlassian.net",
        "your-email@example.com",
        "your-api-token",
        false,
    )
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Search issues
    issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues("project = FOO", true)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Convert to IssuesSet for analysis
    issuesSet, err := issues.IssuesSet(nil)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Print status breakdown
    fmt.Println("Issues by Status:")
    for status, count := range issuesSet.CountsByStatus() {
        fmt.Printf("  %s: %d\n", status, count)
    }

    // Print type breakdown
    fmt.Println("\nIssues by Type:")
    for issueType, count := range issuesSet.CountsByType(true, true) {
        fmt.Printf("  %s: %d\n", issueType, count)
    }
}

docs.title: JQL Builder
docs.text: The root gojira package provides a JQL builder for constructing Jira Query Language queries programmatically.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
import "github.com/grokify/gojira"

jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    StatusesIncl: [][]string{{"Open", "In Progress"}},
}

query := jql.String()
// Result: "project = 'FOO' AND status IN ('Open', 'In Progress')"

docs.title: JQL Structure
docs.text:
The JQL struct supports include and exclude conditions for various fields:

type JQL struct {
    // Date conditions
    CreatedGT       *time.Time
    CreatedGTE      *time.Time
    CreatedLT       *time.Time
    CreatedLTE      *time.Time
    DueGT           *time.Time
    DueGTE          *time.Time
    DueLT           *time.Time
    DueLTE          *time.Time
    UpdatedGT       *time.Time
    UpdatedGTE      *time.Time
    UpdatedLT       *time.Time
    UpdatedLTE      *time.Time

    // String field conditions (outer = AND, inner = IN)
    FiltersIncl     [][]string
    FiltersExcl     [][]string
    IssuesIncl      [][]string
    IssuesExcl      [][]string
    KeysIncl        [][]string
    KeysExcl        [][]string
    LabelsIncl      [][]string
    LabelsExcl      [][]string
    ParentsIncl     [][]string
    ParentsExcl     [][]string
    ProjectsIncl    [][]string
    ProjectsExcl    [][]string
    ResolutionIncl  [][]string
    ResolutionExcl  [][]string
    StatusesIncl    [][]string
    StatusesExcl    [][]string
    TypesIncl       [][]string
    TypesExcl       [][]string

    // Text search
    SummaryLike     []string
    SummaryNotLike  []string
    TextLike        []string
    TextNotLike     []string

    // Custom fields
    CustomFieldIncl map[string][]string
    CustomFieldExcl map[string][]string

    // Raw JQL fragments
    Raw             []string
}

docs.title: Single Project, Single Status
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    StatusesIncl: [][]string{{"Open"}},
}
// project = 'FOO' AND status = 'Open'

docs.title: Multiple Projects
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO", "BAR", "BAZ"}},
}
// project IN ('FOO', 'BAR', 'BAZ')

docs.title: Multiple Status Groups (AND)
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    StatusesIncl: [][]string{
        {"Open", "In Progress"},  // First condition
        {"Blocked"},               // AND this condition
    },
}
// status IN ('Open', 'In Progress') AND status = 'Blocked'

docs.title: Exclude Conditions
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    StatusesExcl: [][]string{{"Done", "Closed"}},
}
// project = 'FOO' AND status NOT IN ('Done', 'Closed')

docs.title: Date Filtering
docs.text:
import "time"

sevenDaysAgo := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -7)
jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    CreatedGTE:   &sevenDaysAgo,
}
// project = 'FOO' AND createdDate >= 2024-01-08

docs.title: Text Search
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    TextLike:     []string{"error"},
}
// project = 'FOO' AND text ~ "error"

docs.title: Summary Search
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    SummaryLike: []string{"login", "authentication"},
}
// summary ~ "login" AND summary ~ "authentication"

docs.title: Custom Fields
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    CustomFieldIncl: map[string][]string{
        "customfield_10001": {"Team A", "Team B"},
    },
}
// cf[10001] IN ('Team A', 'Team B')

docs.title: Raw JQL
docs.text:
For complex conditions not supported by the builder:

jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    Raw: []string{
        "fixVersion = '1.0'",
        "sprint in openSprints()",
    },
}
// project = 'FOO' AND fixVersion = '1.0' AND sprint in openSprints()

docs.title: Labels
docs.text:
jql := gojira.JQL{
    LabelsIncl: [][]string{{"bug", "urgent"}},
}
// labels IN ('bug', 'urgent')

docs.title: Query String
docs.text:
Generate URL-encoded query string:

jql := gojira.JQL{
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
}

qs := jql.QueryString()
// jql=project%20%3D%20%27FOO%27

docs.title: JQL Metadata
docs.text:
Store query metadata for tracking:

jql := gojira.JQL{
    Meta: gojira.JQLMeta{
        Name:        "Open Bugs",
        Description: "All open bugs in FOO project",
        QueryTime:   time.Now(),
    },
    ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO"}},
    TypesIncl:    [][]string{{"Bug"}},
    StatusesIncl: [][]string{{"Open"}},
}

docs.title: Splitting Long Queries
docs.text:
For very long value lists that exceed Jira's limits:

values := []string{"KEY-1", "KEY-2", ..., "KEY-1000"}

jqls := gojira.JQLStringsSimple(
    "key",    // Field name
    false,    // Exclude (false = include)
    values,   // Values
    0,        // Max length (0 = default)
)

// Returns multiple JQL strings, each under the length limit
for _, jql := range jqls {
    issues, _ := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues(jql, false)
    // Process issues...
}

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "log"
    "time"

    "github.com/grokify/gojira"
    "github.com/grokify/gojira/rest"
)

func main() {
    // Build JQL query
    oneMonthAgo := time.Now().AddDate(0, -1, 0)

    jql := gojira.JQL{
        ProjectsIncl: [][]string{{"FOO", "BAR"}},
        TypesIncl:    [][]string{{"Bug", "Task"}},
        StatusesExcl: [][]string{{"Done", "Closed"}},
        CreatedGTE:   &oneMonthAgo,
    }

    query := jql.String()
    fmt.Printf("JQL: %s\n", query)

    // Use with client
    client, err := rest.NewClientFromBasicAuth(
        "https://your-instance.atlassian.net",
        "your-email@example.com",
        "your-api-token",
        false,
    )
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    issues, err := client.IssueAPI.SearchIssues(query, true)
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Found %d issues\n", len(issues))
}
